prerequisites: No account or credentials are required: the openFDA API is a public, read-only API served from the base URL https://api.fda.gov.

This connector is manifest-only and defines no secret fields and no config fields, so there is nothing to configure beyond selecting an openFDA endpoint to read (for example drug, device, or food data).

Optional: a free openFDA API key raises the rate limits from 240 requests/minute and 1,000 requests/day per IP (without a key) to 240 requests/minute and 120,000 requests/day per key. Signing up for a key means agreeing to the openFDA terms of service.

authMethods.name: No authentication (public API)
authMethods.summary: The openFDA API is publicly accessible and requires no credentials. This connector defines no secret fields, so no API key or token is supplied. Requests go directly to https://api.fda.gov and return JSON. An optional free API key (passed as an api_key query parameter) can raise rate limits, but it is not required to use the API.

setupSteps.title: Confirm no credentials are needed
setupSteps.body: The openFDA API is public and read-only. This connector has no secret fields and no config fields, so there are no credentials or settings to enter. The API is reached at the base URL https://api.fda.gov and returns results as JSON.

setupSteps.title: Optional: request a free API key for higher rate limits
setupSteps.body: Without a key, openFDA allows 240 requests per minute and 1,000 requests per day per IP address. To raise the daily limit to 120,000 requests per key (240 requests per minute per key), request a free API key on the openFDA site. The key can be passed as an api_key query parameter. For limits beyond this, openFDA directs users to contact open@fda.hhs.gov.
